name: ExtensionConsent_SourceReference
title: R5: sourceReference
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Consent.sourceReference` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Consent.sourceReference` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Consent,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/DocumentReference,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Contract,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/QuestionnaireResponse)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Consent.sourceReference` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Consent,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/DocumentReference,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Contract,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/QuestionnaireResponse)`
*  R4B: `Consent.source[x]` 0..1 `Attachment, Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Consent,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/DocumentReference,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Contract,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/QuestionnaireResponse)`
*  R4: `Consent.source[x]` 0..1 `Attachment, Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Consent,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/DocumentReference,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Contract,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/QuestionnaireResponse)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Consent.sourceReference` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `Consent.sourceReference` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.source[x]` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The standard extension `alternate-reference` has been mapped as the representation of FHIR R5 element `Consent.sourceReference` with unmapped reference targets: DocumentReference.
Source element `Consent.sourceReference` has unmapped reference types. While the target element `Consent.source[x]` is a choice type and does not allow extensions, the `alternate-reference` extension can be applied to Reference values within it.

element.comment:
Element `Consent.sourceReference` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `Consent.sourceReference` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Consent.source[x]` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The standard extension `alternate-reference` has been mapped as the representation of FHIR R5 element `Consent.sourceReference` with unmapped reference targets: DocumentReference.
Source element `Consent.sourceReference` has unmapped reference types. While the target element `Consent.source[x]` is a choice type and does not allow extensions, the `alternate-reference` extension can be applied to Reference values within it.
The source can be contained inline (Attachment), referenced directly (Consent), referenced in a consent repository (DocumentReference), or simply by an identifier (Identifier), e.g. a CDA document id.

element.short: Source from which this consent is taken
element.definition: A reference to a consent that links back to such a source, a reference to a document repository (e.g. XDS) that stores the original consent document.
element.comment: The source can be contained inline (Attachment), referenced directly (Consent), referenced in a consent repository (DocumentReference), or simply by an identifier (Identifier), e.g. a CDA document id.
